paris.name: Paris
paris.description: Paris, France's capital, is a major European city and a global center for art, fashion, gastronomy, and culture.

nice.name: Nice
nice.description: Nice, located on the French Riviera, offers beautiful beaches, a Mediterranean climate, and a blend of French and Italian culture.

lyon.name: Lyon
lyon.description: Lyon is renowned for its historical architecture, gastronomy, and Renaissance district at the confluence of the Rhône and Saône rivers.

marseille.name: Marseille
marseille.description: Marseille, France's oldest city and largest port, features vibrant multicultural neighborhoods and stunning calanques.

bordeaux.name: Bordeaux
bordeaux.description: Bordeaux, the wine capital of the world, boasts elegant 18th-century architecture, world-class vineyards, and a vibrant riverfront.

strasbourg.name: Strasbourg
strasbourg.description: Strasbourg, located on the German border, blends French and German influences in its cuisine, architecture, and culture. It is also home to important EU institutions.

nantes.name: Nantes
nantes.description: A vibrant city on the Loire River, Nantes is known for its rich maritime history, innovative arts scene, and proximity to the Loire Valley.

montpellier.name: Montpellier
montpellier.description: Montpellier is a vibrant university city with a historic center, elegant squares, and a thriving cultural scene.

lille.name: Lille
lille.description: Lille is a cultural hub in northern France, featuring Flemish architecture, renowned museums, and a lively urban atmosphere.

cannes.name: Cannes
cannes.description: Cannes, the glamorous resort town on the French Riviera, is famous for its international film festival, luxury boutiques, and stunning Mediterranean beaches.

berlin.name: Berlin
berlin.description: Berlin, Germany's dynamic capital, offers a mix of historical landmarks, world-class museums, and a vibrant modern cultural scene.

munich.name: Munich
munich.description: Munich is Bavaria's capital, blending traditional German culture with modern technology and renowned for its Oktoberfest celebrations.

hamburg.name: Hamburg
hamburg.description: Hamburg is Germany's major port city with a rich maritime heritage, impressive Hanseatic architecture, and a lively cultural scene.

cologne.name: Cologne
cologne.description: Cologne is known for its twin-spired Gothic cathedral and a unique blend of Roman heritage and modern cultural vibrancy.

frankfurt.name: Frankfurt
frankfurt.description: Frankfurt is a global financial hub with a striking skyline, international business centers, and a mix of modern and historic architecture.

dusseldorf.name: Düsseldorf
dusseldorf.description: Düsseldorf is a fashion and business center, famous for its shopping boulevard and vibrant arts scene, centered around its charming Altstadt (Old Town).

stuttgart.name: Stuttgart
stuttgart.description: Stuttgart, the cradle of the automobile industry, is home to world-renowned museums like those dedicated to Mercedes-Benz and Porsche.

nuremberg.name: Nuremberg
nuremberg.description: A city steeped in history with medieval walls and architecture, Nuremberg is also famed for its traditional Christmas market.
landmarks: Documentation Centre Nazi Party Rally Grounds

leipzig.name: Leipzig
leipzig.description: Leipzig is a cultural hub with a rich musical heritage, known for its connection to Bach, Mendelssohn, and the peaceful revolution of 1989.

heidelberg.name: Heidelberg
heidelberg.description: Heidelberg is a picturesque university town, famous for its romantic castle ruins and historic old town along the Neckar River.

bremen.name: Bremen
bremen.description: Bremen is a historic Hanseatic city known for its fairytale connection, maritime heritage, and distinctive Gothic architecture.

hannover.name: Hannover
hannover.description: Hannover combines urban innovation with natural beauty, featuring the Royal Gardens of Herrenhausen and a rebuilt historic center.

amsterdam.name: Amsterdam
amsterdam.description: Amsterdam is celebrated for its artistic heritage, elaborate canal system, and narrow houses with gabled facades—a legacy of its 17th-century Golden Age.

rotterdam.name: Rotterdam
rotterdam.description: Rotterdam, home to Europe's largest port, is known for its bold modern architecture, innovative urban design, and energetic atmosphere.

utrecht.name: Utrecht
utrecht.description: Utrecht features a charming medieval center with iconic canals and the towering Dom, creating a vibrant atmosphere fueled by its large student population.

eindhoven.name: Eindhoven
eindhoven.description: A hub for technology and design, Eindhoven is renowned for its innovation, being home to Philips and hosting the annual Dutch Design Week.

maastricht.name: Maastricht
maastricht.description: Maastricht, one of the oldest cities in the Netherlands, is famed for its picturesque squares, historical buildings, and lively culinary scene.

delft.name: Delft
delft.description: Delft is known for its iconic blue pottery, historic canals, and connection to the royal House of Orange-Nassau.

groningen.name: Groningen
groningen.description: Groningen is a vibrant university city in the north, known for its youthful energy, cycling culture, and historic architecture.

haarlem.name: Haarlem
haarlem.description: Haarlem offers historic charm with its medieval buildings, cobblestone streets, and proximity to tulip fields and coastal dunes.

barcelona.name: Barcelona
barcelona.description: Barcelona is a vibrant city known for its unique architecture, particularly the works of Antoni Gaudí, and its rich cultural heritage.

madrid.name: Madrid
madrid.description: Madrid, Spain's capital, offers a mix of elegant boulevards, expansive parks, and world-class museums alongside a buzzing nightlife.

seville.name: Seville
seville.description: Seville captivates with its rich Moorish history, vibrant flamenco culture, and impressive architectural landmarks.

valencia.name: Valencia
valencia.description: Valencia blends futuristic architecture with historic charm, offering beautiful beaches and the birthplace of paella.

granada.name: Granada
granada.description: Granada is known for the stunning Alhambra palace complex, set against the backdrop of the Sierra Nevada mountains.

bilbao.name: Bilbao
bilbao.description: Bilbao, a former industrial city, has transformed into a cultural hub, famous for the titanium-clad Guggenheim Museum.

malaga.name: Malaga
malaga.description: Malaga, Picasso's birthplace, offers Andalusian charm, beautiful beaches, and rich artistic heritage on the Costa del Sol.

rome.name: Rome
rome.description: Rome, steeped in over two millennia of history, offers ancient ruins, Renaissance art, and vibrant street life.

milan.name: Milan
milan.description: Milan is Italy's fashion and design capital, blending historic architecture with modern industry and cultural innovation.

florence.name: Florence
florence.description: Florence is the cradle of the Renaissance, famed for its art, architecture, and enduring cultural legacy.

venice.name: Venice
venice.description: Built on a network of canals, Venice is a city of romance and history, celebrated for its art and unique waterways.

naples.name: Naples
naples.description: Naples offers a vibrant urban life, historical treasures, and serves as the gateway to the Amalfi Coast and Pompeii.

bologna.name: Bologna
bologna.description: Bologna is known for its cuisine, medieval architecture, and the oldest university in the Western world.

palermo.name: Palermo
palermo.description: Palermo, Sicily's capital, showcases a unique blend of Arab-Norman architecture and Mediterranean flair.

bari.name: Bari
bari.description: Bari, the capital of Puglia, is a historic port city known for its medieval old town, vibrant harbor, and authentic Italian culture.

turin.name: Turin
turin.description: Turin combines French-influenced elegance with Italian character, known for its automotive industry and Egyptian Museum.

verona.name: Verona
verona.description: Verona, the setting for Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet, enchants with its Roman arena, medieval squares, and romantic atmosphere.

vienna.name: Vienna
vienna.description: Vienna exudes imperial charm with its classical music legacy, grand palaces, and vibrant coffeehouse culture.

salzburg.name: Salzburg
salzburg.description: Famous as the birthplace of Mozart, Salzburg enchants visitors with its baroque architecture and stunning alpine backdrop.

innsbruck.name: Innsbruck
innsbruck.description: Nestled in the Alps, Innsbruck is renowned for its winter sports and picturesque mountain scenery.

graz.name: Graz
graz.description: Graz, Austria's second-largest city, features a UNESCO-listed Old Town, modern architecture, and a thriving university culture.

linz.name: Linz
linz.description: Linz has transformed from an industrial city to a cultural center, known for its electronic arts festival and riverside views.

brussels.name: Brussels
brussels.description: Brussels is the de facto capital of the European Union, celebrated for its historical sites, cuisine, and comic art culture.

antwerp.name: Antwerp
antwerp.description: Antwerp is a vibrant port city with a rich history in diamond trade, art, and fashion.

bruges.name: Bruges
bruges.description: Bruges enchants visitors with its medieval architecture, romantic canals, and cobblestone streets that evoke a fairytale atmosphere.

ghent.name: Ghent
ghent.description: Ghent offers a perfect blend of historic charm and youthful energy, with its medieval architecture and vibrant university atmosphere.

liege.name: Liège
liege.description: Liège combines industrial heritage with cultural richness, set in the scenic valley of the Meuse River.

copenhagen.name: Copenhagen
copenhagen.description: Copenhagen combines historic charm with modern design, known for its cycling culture, sustainable living, and hygge lifestyle.

aarhus.name: Aarhus
aarhus.description: Aarhus blends ancient Viking history with modern innovation, featuring cutting-edge museums and a bustling waterfront.

odense.name: Odense
odense.description: Odense, the birthplace of Hans Christian Andersen, charms with its literary heritage, cobblestone streets, and historic homes.

dublin.name: Dublin
dublin.description: Dublin is a lively city known for its literary history, vibrant pub culture, and iconic landmarks.

cork.name: Cork
cork.description: Cork, Ireland's second city, offers a vibrant arts scene, maritime heritage, and serves as a gateway to the scenic southwest.

galway.name: Galway
galway.description: Galway captivates with its bohemian spirit, traditional Irish music, and stunning location on the Wild Atlantic Way.

limerick.name: Limerick
limerick.description: Limerick combines medieval history with Georgian elegance, situated along the majestic River Shannon.

lisbon.name: Lisbon
lisbon.description: Lisbon, perched on seven hills, offers a blend of traditional heritage and striking modernism, with a scenic riverside setting.

porto.name: Porto
porto.description: Porto is known for its historic center, vibrant wine culture, and dramatic riverside landscapes.

faro.name: Faro
faro.description: Faro, the gateway to the Algarve, combines a historic old town with nearby golden beaches and natural reserves.

coimbra.name: Coimbra
coimbra.description: Coimbra, home to one of Europe's oldest universities, offers rich academic traditions, medieval architecture, and Fado music.

funchal.name: Funchal
funchal.description: Funchal, the capital of Madeira Island, features subtropical gardens, volcanic landscapes, and a picturesque harbor.

athens.name: Athens
athens.description: Athens is a city where ancient history meets modern urban life, boasting iconic ruins and lively neighborhoods.

thessaloniki.name: Thessaloniki
thessaloniki.description: Thessaloniki blends Byzantine heritage with a vibrant waterfront and cultural scene as Greece's second-largest city.

heraklion.name: Heraklion
heraklion.description: Heraklion, Crete's capital, combines ancient Minoan heritage with a lively modern atmosphere.

rhodes.name: Rhodes
rhodes.description: Rhodes Town features a UNESCO-listed medieval Old Town enclosed within impressive fortress walls.

santorini.name: Santorini
santorini.description: Santorini captivates with its iconic white-washed buildings perched on volcanic cliffs overlooking the azure Aegean Sea.

stockholm.name: Stockholm
stockholm.description: Stockholm is built on a series of islands and is renowned for its stunning waterfront, historic core, and innovative design scene.

gothenburg.name: Gothenburg
gothenburg.description: Gothenburg offers maritime charm with its harbor, canals, and relaxed atmosphere as Sweden's second-largest city.

malmo.name: Malmö
malmo.description: Malmö combines historic roots with modern sustainability, connected to Copenhagen by the Öresund Bridge.

uppsala.name: Uppsala
uppsala.description: Uppsala, Sweden's fourth-largest city, is known for its prestigious university, Gothic cathedral, and Viking heritage.

oslo.name: Oslo
oslo.description: Oslo offers a unique blend of modern architecture and natural beauty, set against a backdrop of fjords and forested hills.

bergen.name: Bergen
bergen.description: Bergen enchants with its colorful wooden houses along the old wharf, surrounded by seven mountains and fjords.

trondheim.name: Trondheim
trondheim.description: Trondheim, Norway's third-largest city and former capital, features colorful warehouses and a stunning Gothic cathedral.

tromso.name: Tromsø
tromso.description: Tromsø, located above the Arctic Circle, offers northern lights viewing, midnight sun, and a vibrant cultural scene.

stavanger.name: Stavanger
stavanger.description: Stavanger blends old-world charm with modern energy, serving as the gateway to spectacular fjords and the famous Pulpit Rock.

helsinki.name: Helsinki
helsinki.description: Helsinki is known for its design-driven culture, maritime charm, and a mix of modern and neoclassical architecture.

tampere.name: Tampere
tampere.description: Tampere, located between two lakes, was a historical industrial center that has transformed into a hub for culture and technology.

turku.name: Turku
turku.description: Turku, Finland's oldest city and former capital, offers medieval heritage, an archipelago, and a lively cultural scene.

zurich.name: Zurich
zurich.description: Zurich is Switzerland's financial center, offering a mix of medieval charm, modern amenities, and lakeside beauty.

geneva.name: Geneva
geneva.description: Geneva, a global hub for diplomacy, combines French influence, a magnificent lakefront, and mountain views.

basel.name: Basel
basel.description: Basel, at the meeting point of three countries, is renowned for its art, architecture, and pharmaceutical industry.

bern.name: Bern
bern.description: Bern, Switzerland's capital, charms with its medieval old town, covered arcades, and scenic Aare River.

lucerne.name: Lucerne
lucerne.description: Lucerne offers picture-perfect lake and mountain scenery, with its iconic wooden bridge and well-preserved medieval core.

lausanne.name: Lausanne
lausanne.description: Lausanne, home to the International Olympic Committee, offers spectacular views of Lake Geneva and the Alps.

prague.name: Prague
prague.description: Prague is famous for its well-preserved medieval architecture, enchanting bridges, and vibrant cultural scene.

brno.name: Brno
brno.description: Brno, the Czech Republic's second-largest city, is the capital of Moravia region and a gateway to South Moravian wine country. Known for UNESCO World Heritage Villa Tugendhat, historic Špilberk Castle
landmarks: Cathedral of St. Peter and Paul

pilsen.name: Pilsen
pilsen.description: Pilsen is renowned worldwide for its beer brewing tradition and offers a charming historic center.

cesky-krumlov.name: Český Krumlov
cesky-krumlov.description: Český Krumlov is a fairytale-like town with a stunning castle complex and winding riverside setting.

olomouc.name: Olomouc
olomouc.description: Olomouc, a historic Moravian city, features the UNESCO-listed Holy Trinity Column and beautiful baroque architecture. Known for its university atmosphere and authentic Czech character.

ostrava.name: Ostrava
ostrava.description: Ostrava, Czech Republic's third-largest city, has transformed from an industrial center into a vibrant cultural hub known for its unique post-industrial architecture and mining heritage.

hradec-kralove.name: Hradec Králové
hradec-kralove.description: Hradec Králové, known as the 'Queen of Czech Cities,' combines Gothic and modern architecture in a beautiful setting at the confluence of two rivers.

liberec.name: Liberec
liberec.description: Liberec, set in the foothills of the Sudeten Mountains, is known for its textile heritage, winter sports, and proximity to stunning natural landscapes.

budapest.name: Budapest
budapest.description: Budapest is split by the Danube River into two distinct areas—Buda and Pest—each with its own historical and cultural identity.

debrecen.name: Debrecen
debrecen.description: Debrecen, Hungary's second-largest city, features a central Great Reformed Church and rich cultural institutions.

pécs.name: Pécs
pécs.description: Pécs blends 2,000 years of history with a Mediterranean atmosphere and UNESCO-listed early Christian sites.

warsaw.name: Warsaw
warsaw.description: Warsaw, the capital of Poland, is a dynamic blend of modern skyscrapers and meticulously reconstructed historic districts.

krakow.name: Krakow
krakow.description: Krakow charms visitors with its well-preserved medieval core, vibrant cultural scene, and rich historical heritage.

gdansk.name: Gdańsk
gdansk.description: Gdańsk is a Baltic port city with a Hanseatic heritage, striking brick architecture, and historical significance.

wroclaw.name: Wrocław
wroclaw.description: Wrocław is built on multiple islands connected by bridges, featuring Gothic architecture and a vibrant university atmosphere.

poznan.name: Poznań
poznan.description: Poznań combines a charming old town with industrial heritage and is known for its international trade fairs.
landmarks: Cathedral of St. Peter and Paul

split.name: Split
split.description: Split is celebrated for the monumental Diocletian's Palace, which forms the heart of this vibrant coastal city.

zagreb.name: Zagreb
zagreb.description: Zagreb, Croatia's capital, blends Austro-Hungarian architecture with a vibrant café culture and artistic spirit.

zadar.name: Zadar
zadar.description: Zadar combines ancient Roman ruins with innovative modern installations along its scenic Adriatic waterfront.

rijeka.name: Rijeka
rijeka.description: Rijeka, Croatia's primary seaport, features Habsburg-era architecture, industrial heritage, and a multicultural identity.

tallinn.name: Tallinn
tallinn.description: Tallinn boasts one of Europe's best-preserved medieval city centers alongside cutting-edge technology and design.

tartu.name: Tartu
tartu.description: Tartu, Estonia's intellectual center, offers a rich university heritage, bohemian atmosphere, and historic wooden architecture.

riga.name: Riga
riga.description: Riga features the largest collection of Art Nouveau buildings in Europe alongside a charming medieval Old Town.

jurmala.name: Jūrmala
jurmala.description: Jūrmala is a resort city known for its wooden Art Nouveau villas, long sandy beach, and spa traditions.

vilnius.name: Vilnius
vilnius.description: Vilnius offers one of Eastern Europe's largest baroque old towns, featuring diverse architectural styles and artistic atmosphere.

kaunas.name: Kaunas
kaunas.description: Kaunas blends modernist architecture with medieval history and served as Lithuania's temporary capital between the wars.

klaipeda.name: Klaipėda
klaipeda.description: Klaipėda is Lithuania's main seaport, offering a distinctive German-influenced old town and gateway to the Curonian Spit.

ljubljana.name: Ljubljana
ljubljana.description: Ljubljana captivates with its riverside cafés, distinctive bridges, and harmonious blend of baroque and Art Nouveau architecture.

maribor.name: Maribor
maribor.description: Maribor, Slovenia's second-largest city, is known for the world's oldest grapevine and its picturesque Drava River setting.

bled.name: Bled
bled.description: Bled is renowned for its stunning alpine lake with a fairy-tale island church and dramatic castle perched on a cliff.

bratislava.name: Bratislava
bratislava.description: Bratislava combines a charming old town with contemporary urban development along the Danube River.

kosice.name: Košice
kosice.description: Košice features a well-preserved medieval core centered around a magnificent Gothic cathedral and lively main street.

reykjavik.name: Reykjavik
reykjavik.description: Reykjavik, Iceland's vibrant capital, offers a unique blend of Nordic charm, geothermal wonders, and stunning natural landscapes.

tirana.name: Tirana
tirana.description: Albania's vibrant capital blends Ottoman heritage with colorful communist-era buildings and buzzing café culture at the foot of Mount Dajti.

durres.name: Durres
durres.description: Ancient port city on the Adriatic coast, home to a stunning Roman amphitheater and beautiful beaches backed by a rich trading history.

sarajevo.name: Sarajevo
sarajevo.description: A crossroads of civilizations where Ottoman bazaars, Austro-Hungarian architecture, and poignant war history create a deeply moving destination.

mostar.name: Mostar
mostar.description: Famous for its iconic Ottoman bridge, Stari Most, arching over the emerald Neretva River amid a charming old town of cobblestone streets.

sofia.name: Sofia
sofia.description: Bulgaria's capital offers an eclectic mix of Byzantine churches, Soviet monuments, and Ottoman mosques set against the backdrop of Mount Vitosha.

plovdiv.name: Plovdiv
plovdiv.description: One of Europe's oldest continuously inhabited cities, featuring a stunning Roman theater, colorful Revival-era houses, and a thriving arts scene.

varna.name: Varna
varna.description: Bulgaria's seaside gem on the Black Sea coast, known for its golden beaches, ancient Thracian gold treasures, and lively summer atmosphere.

london.name: London
london.description: London, the capital of England and the UK, is a global powerhouse of history, royalty, theatre, free museums, and food from every corner of the world.
